description: Implement Tier 2 features: Spread/slippage-aware entry gating (EA writes spread, Python reads and gates), microstructure features integration into model input, correlation regime detection (DXY/bonds/equities state), and adaptive confidence threshold that self-tunes based on recent accuracy.
steps: Create python_bridge_v3/data/spread_monitor.py: A SpreadMonitor class that reads SPREAD_FILE CSV (format: timestamp,spread_points,ask,bid written by EA every tick). Methods: get_current_spread() -> float; get_average_spread(window=100) -> float; is_spread_acceptable() -> bool (True if current spread < max_spread_multiplier * average); get_spread_features() -> dict with spread_ratio, spread_percentile, spread_velocity for model input.

Update python_bridge_v3/data/microstructure.py: Add method get_feature_vector() that returns a flat numpy array of all microstructure features suitable for concatenation with existing model input features. Features: [tick_rate_normalized, bid_ask_bounce_rate, large_order_flow_normalized, spread_velocity, volume_acceleration, tick_imbalance_ratio]. Ensure the array length is fixed (pad with zeros if data unavailable).

Create python_bridge_v3/strategies/correlation_regime.py: A CorrelationRegimeDetector class. Uses cross-pair data (DXY, bonds/TNX, equities/SPY) to detect correlation regime states. Methods: update(pair_data: dict) -> updates internal state with latest cross-pair prices; get_regime() -> returns one of 'risk_on', 'risk_off', 'dollar_driven', 'decorrelated'; get_confidence_adjustment(signal_direction) -> float adjustment to confidence based on correlation state (e.g., risk_off + BUY gold = +0.05 boost, dollar_driven + strong_dxy + BUY gold = -0.10 penalty); compute_features() -> np.ndarray of correlation features for model input. Use rolling correlations between gold and DXY, gold and TNX, gold and SPY over configurable window.

Create python_bridge_v3/strategies/adaptive_threshold.py: An AdaptiveConfidenceThreshold class. Tracks recent trade outcomes and adjusts the min_confidence threshold dynamically. Methods: record_trade(confidence_at_entry, won: bool) -> stores trade; get_current_threshold() -> float (the dynamic threshold); _recalculate() -> after each trade, computes optimal threshold by finding the confidence level above which win_rate > target (default 55%). If recent accuracy is high (>60%), lower threshold to get more trades. If accuracy is low (<45%), raise threshold to be more selective. Bounds: min_threshold=0.15, max_threshold=0.55. Rate of change limited to adjustment_rate (2%) per trade to prevent whiplash.

Update python_bridge_v3/strategies/signal_generator.py: (1) After computing momentum direction, check spread_monitor.is_spread_acceptable() - if False, return HOLD with log message 'spread too wide'. (2) Add microstructure features to the feature vector passed to ensemble (concatenate microstructure.get_feature_vector() as additional input dimensions or as a separate signal modifier). (3) Apply correlation_regime.get_confidence_adjustment() to timing_confidence. (4) Replace the fixed min_confidence with adaptive_threshold.get_current_threshold() when enable_adaptive_threshold is True. (5) After each trade closes, call adaptive_threshold.record_trade().

Update python_bridge_v3/main.py: Initialize SpreadMonitor, CorrelationRegimeDetector, AdaptiveConfidenceThreshold. Pass them to SignalGenerator or call them in run_cycle. Feed cross-pair data to CorrelationRegimeDetector each cycle. Wire adaptive threshold feedback from trade confirmations.
acceptance_criteria: python_bridge_v3/data/spread_monitor.py exists with SpreadMonitor class implementing get_current_spread(), get_average_spread(), is_spread_acceptable(), get_spread_features()
python_bridge_v3/data/microstructure.py has get_feature_vector() returning fixed-size numpy array
python_bridge_v3/strategies/adaptive_threshold.py exists with AdaptiveConfidenceThreshold class that self-tunes
signal_generator.py checks spread before allowing signal, applies correlation adjustment, uses adaptive threshold
main.py initializes and wires all Tier 2 components behind enable_* flags
All imports work cleanly without errors
verification: Run: python -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'python_bridge_v3'); from data.spread_monitor import SpreadMonitor; from strategies.correlation_regime import CorrelationRegimeDetector; from strategies.adaptive_threshold import AdaptiveConfidenceThreshold; print('All Tier 2 modules import OK')"

Run: python -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'python_bridge_v3'); from strategies.adaptive_threshold import AdaptiveConfidenceThreshold; at = AdaptiveConfidenceThreshold(); at.record_trade(0.4, True); at.record_trade(0.3, False); print(f'Threshold: {at.get_current_threshold():.3f}'); print('Adaptive threshold works')"
Run: python -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'python_bridge_v3'); from main import PythonMLBridge; print('Full v3 import OK')"
